title: My Top 10 Food Hacks at Costco on a Budget

chunks.title: Introduction to Costco Food Hacks
chunks.summary: The video introduces viewers to ten great food hacks for shopping at Costco, particularly focusing on healthy animal-based and organic options. The presenter highlights how these hacks can enhance your shopping experience.
chunks.transcript: I know you guys love Costco. In this video, I'm going to show you my 10 best Costco food hacks. You can actually find some pretty decent food at Costco, and I'm going to show it all to you. I'm gatekeeping nothing. 10 hacks at Costco that are going to blow your mind when you're shopping for healthy animal-based organic food. Let's go. All right, guys.

chunks.title: Costco's Ribeye Steaks
chunks.summary: The first hack focuses on the availability of USDA Prime ribeye steaks at Costco for a significantly lower price. The presenter emphasizes the value of these steaks compared to other grocery stores.
chunks.transcript: Hack number one. This is the biggest one. Check this out. You can get ribe eyes at Costco in the Primal for $19 a pound. This is USDA prime. It's not grass-fed. They don't have any primals or grass-fed steaks. Regular beef is still nutritious for humans. I'm a huge fan of grass-fed beef. But this is amazing because the regular ribe eyes here are now $27 a pound, $28 a pound sometimes. So 19 bucks a pound for ribe eyes. You got a party, you want to celebrate, whatever. If you bring this thing home, boom, and throw it on the counter, your family's going to be so stoked. So that's hack number one, guys. Don't sleep on the primals at Costco.

chunks.title: Frozen Organic Berries
chunks.summary: The presenter shares a great deal on frozen organic strawberries, explaining the advantages of frozen berries over fresh ones in terms of nutrient retention. This is highlighted as a must-buy hack.
chunks.transcript: Okay, guys. Hack number five. Costco has pretty darn good deals on frozen organic berries, especially frozen organic strawberries. So, these are $3.20 a pound. I can get four pounds of strawberries for $12.79. Why do I like frozen berries? They're often picked pretty close to optimal ripeness because they can be flash frozen. Fresh berries sometimes are picked before they're ripe. They ripen on the shelf. You're going to have a little different nutrient profile in a fruit when it's picked at optimal ripeness. You guys know this. When you get a strawberry and it's red and really luscious on the vine or you get a really red tomato off the vine, that's when it's the best. When you pick it early and it's green and it ripens on the shelf or they spray it with ethylene gas, not ideal. Look, I have some balance of fresh and frozen fruit in my diet. I use fruit in my morning smoothies. I also put all kinds of stuff like raw milk, hardened soil, whole package in my morning smoothies. Lineage, glyphosate, raw organic honey in my morning smoothies. I've shown you guys this. It's a powerful smoothie. But frozen berries are great for that and this is an incredible deal. So, this is a huge hack at Costco not to miss.

chunks.title: Understanding Costco Pricing
chunks.summary: In this segment, the presenter explains how to decode Costco’s pricing to determine which items are on sale. Specific price endings indicate different types of sales and promotions.
chunks.transcript: hack. Hack number eight, guys. This blew my mind. You can tell a lot about whether an item is on sale just by the price at Costco. So, if the price ends in N9, that's a normal pricing. Nothing special. 0.97 is actually manager special at Costco. This means it's on sale. Well, they don't tell you it's on sale. So, 0.97 is probably the rarest thing. And these guava fruit bites, which I wouldn't eat. These are on sale from the Costco manager. The 49, the 39 are manufacturers incentive. chunks.title: Cookware Options at Costco
chunks.summary: This hack highlights the availability of quality stainless steel cookware at Costco. The presenter advises against non-stick cookware due to potential harmful chemicals, suggesting safer alternatives.
chunks.transcript: you just know what the numbers at the end of these things mean. Hack number nine, Costco actually sells good cookware. I would go for the Hankles stainless steel cookware. It's kind of like the BPA thing, guys. A lot of water bottles now that are plastic say it's BPA free, but then they have BPS and BPE.
